Jobs | Sociobiology

Making the Right Moves | HHMI.org
Twelve easy steps to embrace or avoid scientific petrification: lessons learned from a career in otolith research† | ICES Journal of Marine Science | Oxford Academic
Career Options for Scientists
What your PI forgot to tell you: why you actually might want a job running a research lab
Tips for Young Scientists on the Junior Faculty/Independent Investigator Job Search: Neuron
Auswahl von Forschern: Wie sich die Qualität der Auswahl verbessern lässt
Choosing a Good Mentor for Scientists
Use The CommKit : Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard
Tips for negotiating salary and startup for newly-hired tenure-track faculty | Dynamic Ecology
Career Tools | Burroughs Wellcome Fund
Job-Seq: The Lowest Throughput Sequencing Technology
Finding a lab to do your postdoc - Google Docs
How to Pick a Graduate Advisor: Neuron
Postdocs’ advice on pursuing a research career in academia: A qualitative analysis of free-text survey responses

twitter

Masahiro Ryo on Twitter: "10 tips for academic job application: I recently joined a selection committee. I learned a lot by observing candidates. Combined with my experience, let me share my personal ideas about academic job applications for early career researchers. Could be wrong, but hoping it helps." / Twitter
Igor Ulitsky on Twitter: "Since this came up on my feed, here's my doctrine for finding a postdoc (in biology): You need to aim to interview at five labs, ideally from 2-3 (sub-)fields, and ideally at ~same time to decide quickly. To do so, make a list of 20, shortlist to 8-10 1/n" / Twitter
Brea Hampton-Brown on Twitter: "Starting the last year of my PhD has me thinking about where to go for a postdoc. How did you decide where to postdoc? Science? Mentorship? Location? #AcademicTwitter #blackinstem #BlackintheIvory" / Twitter
wyomingwormboy on Twitter: "Some (hopefully not too late) thoughts for students about choosing a dissertation lab. A tangled🧵 One of the trickier aspects of being the Director of an umbrella grad program for 10 years was advising students (when asked) about choosing a lab at the end of their rotations. 1/8" / Twitter

Guide to a Postdoc
Academic job hunting: resources and advice | Dimitar Kostadinov
50 COMMON INTERVIEW Q&A | Bhuvana Sundaramoorthy\'s Blog
10 Most Asked Interview Questions: How to Tackle Them - Hongkiat
Cover letter for a job or internship position : NSE Communication Lab
The Commandments of Cover Letter Creation | Science | AAAS

Kill Ideas Quickly – Natural selection is why you need to work so hard as a scientist – The Gregg Lab
How Life Sciences Actually Work: Findings of a Year-Long Investigation - Alexey Guzey
The importance of stupidity in scientific research | Journal of Cell Science
How to choose a good scientific problem. - PubMed - NCBI
Renaissance minds in 21st century science | Genome Biology | Full Text
Philosophy of Biology: The analysis of living systems can generate both knowledge and illusions | eLife
Modest Advice | Stearns Lab
REPLY TO STEARNS - reply.pdf
A hypothesis is a liability | Genome Biology | Full Text
Shengwu Li on Twitter: "Thoughts on learning to "fail fast" in research. When you have a new idea, and the project is worthwhile only if P, then you need to check P first, not last. Examples: 1. You can only establish causality with a certain kind of data. 🧵" / Twitter
Novel predictions arise from contradictions | Genome Biology | Full Text
Theory in Biology: Figure 1 or Figure 7?: Trends in Cell Biology
Improvisational science | Genome Biology | Full Text
Make science disruptive again | Nature Biotechnology
Planning Your Scientific Journey • iBiology

5

Anne Silverman on Twitter: "In working as an associate editor for the past few years, I find the “curtain was pulled back” on many aspects of the publication process. My behavior as an author and a reviewer changed because of being an editor. How you ask? 🧵" / Twitter
Microsoft Word - Science of Writing.rtf - gopen_and_swan_science_of_scientific_writing.pdf
Rule of Three: advice on writing a PhD thesis – quantixed
DORA – San Francisco Declaration on Research Assessment (DORA)
A uniform format for manuscript submission: Cell
Dr. Katie Grogan 👩‍🔬🧬 on Twitter: "Yesterday I offered to share some writing advice that has worked for me, &amp; it garnered a lot of interest! So I'm going to share it here in a thread instead of individually by email because we can all agree that academic writing is HARD! So here we go #phdchat #AcademicTwitter" / Twitter
Ashley Ross on Twitter: "I've been asked to do this before &amp; I'll be honest, I feel like an imposter but here it goes. Ashley's tips on writing an NIH grant (thread) 1. The 1st and most obvious tip is to pick something that you are excited about. Trust me, the reviewers can tell if you aren't excited." / Twitter

2c_Summary_para.pdf
JEFworks Lab
Rita Strack on Twitter: "Finally as promised! This is a #ritaunsolicited very long thread on writing effective abstracts for methods papers. (thread 1/2)" / Twitter
Rita Strack on Twitter: "#ritaunsolicited thread 2/2 on abstracts for methods papers begins here! Please find 1/2 first if you're interested, or this will make almost no sense :D" / Twitter
Anna Clemens, PhD on Twitter: "🧵 Are you and your co-authors editing the abstract of your paper again and again before submitting to get it just perfect? 👇 Use this template instead and save yourself hoursss! #PostDoc #ECRchat #AcademicChater" / Twitter

LAB CULTURE

Science, narcissism and the quest for visibility - Lemaitre - 2017 - The FEBS Journal - Wiley Online Library
The key to a happy lab life is in the manual
The Thrill of Defeat - Issue 72: Quandary - Nautilus
RajLab: I need a coach
How Life Sciences Actually Work: Findings of a Year-Long Investigation - Alexey Guzey
Laboratory Management Tips, Articles | Lab Manager
How to grow a healthy lab
Ten simple rules towards healthier research labs
100 research rules of the game: How to make your research world class; how to successfully publish in top international refereed journals | Emerald Insight
Staffing the Lab | Burroughs Wellcome Fund
Supervising PhD Students: A practical guide and toolkit
Lab Values – Bolnick Lab at the University of Connecticut
Health tips for research groups

Lab Policy | Carpenter Lab
A Guide to Setting Up and Managing a Lab at a Research-Intensive Institution[v1] | Preprints

via Twitter

Daniel Bolnick on Twitter: "What's 1 lab management trick you PIs use that works really well for you? Mine: Everyone, every week, emails the group a description of (1) what they did last week, (2) goals for the next week, and (3) something interesting they are reading. (Answers may be collated in a blog)" / Twitter
Mark Charbonneau 🌈👨‍🔬 on Twitter: "The job market is extremely hot right now, and recruiters will be calling you weekly (if not daily). If you change roles, do it for the right reasons. “Hopping” every 1-2 years might net you more money in the short run, but there is an opportunity cost. (6/)" / Twitter
Mark Charbonneau 🌈👨‍🔬 on Twitter: "The questions I get most often from PhD students are about what’s is like working in biotech. So, here are some of my observations on the industry that I wish I’d known in grad school (Note that everyone’s experience is different, so YMMV). #biotech #career (1/)" / Twitter
Prof. Janet E. Hill on Twitter: "An annual ritual completed! Updated the lab Gantt chart. Great memory aid for me and a fun way for current students to see how they fit into the story of the group. https://t.co/Z3ZlGAjXHO" / Twitter
Kafui Dzirasa, MD PhD on Twitter: "I've rebranded my lab. Why, you ask?!? Because my younger brother, founder of the now most profitable minority owned small business in Baltimore, made me watch this clip. https://t.co/zKyZfN0Ztz" / Twitter
Ben Martin on Twitter: "Anyone have an electronic lab notebook that they love? A bit overwhelmed with options as I try to catch up with the 21st century 😉" / Twitter
Sonenberg Lab on Twitter: "The Wisdom of Nahum for the New Millenium. 10 rules we should all abide by :) https://t.co/SC1Ofep2op" / Twitter
Dr. ᐯᗩᒪEᖇIE ᕼOᖇᔕᒪEY 🔬 on Twitter: "Speaking to faculty candidates and new faculty recently reminded me of the first few years of starting my lab. Some advice 🧵 1/ #newPI #sciencetwitter #AcademicTwitter #phdchat #advice" / Twitter
Piali Sengupta on Twitter: "Back to this. Electronic lab notebooks. We are super disorganized and past time to deal with our scattered images, analyses, sequences etc. Current faves? Current non-faves? Thanks #ScienceTwitter" / Twitter
Iain Cheeseman on Twitter: "With the new academic year starting, and many people beginning fresh as graduate students, post-docs, or junior faculty, I have been thinking a lot about the core philosophies that govern my own perspectives on science. So I thought I would share. Welcome to The Tao of Cheese" / Twitter

Early Career Researcher Toolbox: Free Online Molecular Biology Tools
What’s a failed experiment?. This question came up after a student… | by KamounLab | Oct, 2021 | Medium
Denis Wirtz on Twitter: "I have been trying to predict the degree of success of PhD students for a long time. Success takes creativity, hard work, writing and presentation skills, luck. But I think one of the very best predictors of success is critically reading the literature. What counts the most?" / Twitter
Lyrissa Barnett Lidsky on Twitter: "Ten pieces of advice that have helped me at various times. 1. Institutions don’t love you back. If you disappear tomorrow the institution will go on." / Twitter
The Making of a Scientist | Nature
How I learned to nurture relationships in academia—and be more kind | Science | AAAS
Petr Svoboda on X: "I'm putting together a material on mentoring PhD students. I compiled features of good mentors from old stuff &amp; experience (thank you @FMIscience!), so below is what I have. I look for comments. Anything is missing? Add it to the thread. Disagree with some point? Comment it." / X

For Reviewers - PLOS
Marc Coutanche on Twitter: "Sharing my response to a recent review request from Nature Neuroscience. If you submit to a Nature journal, your set of reviewers may not be as specialized or matched to topic as at other journals (due to the smaller pool of reviewers that Nature now has access to) https://t.co/Kv8fsEKvXU" / Twitter
Anne Silverman on Twitter: "In working as an associate editor for the past few years, I find the “curtain was pulled back” on many aspects of the publication process. My behavior as an author and a reviewer changed because of being an editor. How you ask? 🧵" / Twitter

via Twitter

Itai Yanai on Twitter: "Learning how to give a good talk is crucial, but we don't teach it as much as we should. Here are the suggestions I give my student about the right kind of preparation, execution, and question-answering approach. https://t.co/nTpa70MGTR" / Twitter
Jasper Polak on Twitter: "McKinsey makes $500,000+ on a single presentation. You can learn their simple framework below (for free):" / Twitter
Will Ratcliff on Twitter: "I teach a class on how to give a great scientific talk. The core idea is that humans absorb information effortlessly if it is delivered as a… https://t.co/nMcoZCrz1j"
Tim Stearns on Twitter: "@MahjoubLab @TanentzapfLab Originally formulated by Charlie Anderson when he was a TA, it's even easier to remember as QUERY: QUestion Experiment Result Your interpretation We included it in a paper about a lab course: https://t.co/4MGDEUUH2y. It really does work to make clearer presentations." / Twitter
